questions.question_text: Which statement best describes health in health: the ultimate treasure?

options.text: A balanced diet supplies needed nutrients and energy in suitable proportions.
Health is a state of physical, mental, and social well-being, not merely the absence of disease.
Nutrients are substances in food that the body needs for energy, growth, repair, and regulation.
A nutrient deficiency occurs when the body does not receive enough of a needed nutrient over time.

questions.explanation: Health is a state of physical, mental, and social well-being, not merely the absence of disease.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 3; original question derived from the chapter concept manifest

questions.question_text: Which example is most directly associated with health?

options.text: Carbohydrates can provide energy while proteins support growth and repair.
A meal can combine grains, pulses, vegetables, fruit, and other locally suitable foods.
Rest, supportive relationships, and nutritious food can all affect health.
Insufficient iron intake can contribute to anaemia.

questions.explanation: Rest, supportive relationships, and nutritious food can all affect health.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 3; original question derived from the chapter concept manifest

questions.question_text: Which statement best distinguishes health from a nearby idea?

options.text: A deficiency is linked to inadequate nutrient supply, not to a food being unfamiliar.
Balanced means varied and adequate, not simply eating a large amount of one food.
Nutrients are components of food; foods can contain more than one nutrient.
Health includes well-being and functioning, while disease is a condition that may disturb them.

questions.explanation: Health includes well-being and functioning, while disease is a condition that may disturb them.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 3; original question derived from the chapter concept manifest

questions.question_text: Which situation best applies the idea of health?

options.text: Plan a routine that balances food, sleep, physical activity, and emotional support.
Evaluate a meal by looking for different food groups and reasonable portions.
Match a food choice to the nutrient need it can help meet.
Use a varied diet and qualified health advice to reduce deficiency risk.

questions.explanation: Plan a routine that balances food, sleep, physical activity, and emotional support.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 3; original question derived from the chapter concept manifest

questions.question_text: Which statement corrects a common misunderstanding about health?

options.text: A costly or fashionable food is not automatically part of a balanced diet.
A person who has no diagnosed disease may still need support for overall well-being.
No single nutrient performs every body function.
One symptom alone cannot diagnose a deficiency.

questions.explanation: A person who has no diagnosed disease may still need support for overall well-being.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 3; original question derived from the chapter concept manifest

questions.question_text: Which statement best describes communicable diseases in health: the ultimate treasure?

options.text: Pathogens are disease-causing organisms or infectious agents.

questions.question_text: Which statement best distinguishes communicable diseases from a nearby idea?

options.text: Public health acts at community scale, while personal habits act at individual scale.

Non-communicable describes transmission, not whether prevention or treatment is possible.
Vaccines support prevention; they are not a treatment for every illness after it begins.

questions.explanation: Vaccines support prevention; they are not a treatment for every illness after it begins.

questions.question_text: Which situation best applies the idea of immunity and vaccines?

options.text: Follow the recommended vaccination schedule and seek medical guidance when needed.
Identify the route of spread and select a hygiene practice that interrupts it.
Use regular activity, nutritious food, and professional advice to manage risk.
Combine personal hygiene with community measures such as safe water and waste management.

questions.explanation: Follow the recommended vaccination schedule and seek medical guidance when needed.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 3; original question derived from the chapter concept manifest

questions.question_text: Which statement corrects a common misunderstanding about immunity and vaccines?

options.text: Using more soap than necessary does not replace thorough washing.
Vaccination does not mean a person can ignore every other preventive measure.
A disease being non-communicable does not make it unimportant.
Individual action alone cannot solve every public-health problem.

questions.explanation: Vaccination does not mean a person can ignore every other preventive measure.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 3; original question derived from the chapter concept manifest

questions.question_text: A learner is explaining immunity and vaccines. Which description is most accurate?

options.text: A non-communicable disease does not spread from one person to another as an infection.
Hygiene practices reduce exposure to harmful agents and interrupt routes of infection.
The immune system helps the body recognise and respond to disease-causing agents.
Public health protects communities through prevention, safe environments, and access to care.

questions.explanation: The immune system helps the body recognise and respond to disease-causing agents.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 3; original question derived from the chapter concept manifest

questions.question_text: Which observation would provide useful evidence about immunity and vaccines?

options.text: Clean water systems and vaccination programmes protect many people at once.
Washing hands before eating can reduce transfer from hands to food.
Some long-term heart conditions are influenced by biology and lifestyle rather than person-to-person spread.
Vaccination prepares immune memory against a specific disease threat.

questions.explanation: Vaccination prepares immune memory against a specific disease threat.
